hypothesis: If open-weight language models are first supervised fine-tuned on BCC/B2 composition triples and then optimized with Direct Preference Optimization using thermodynamic-simulation-based rewards (with β=0.5), then they will generate alloy candidates with higher mean hierarchical reward, greater compositional validity, improved coverage and novelty relative to the Materials Project, and higher unique-generation rates than SFT-only models and baseline methods, because DPO aligns model outputs to physics-based synthesis objectives while β-regularization mitigates distribution shift.
